opencodeCommands.name: openagents-review
opencodeCommands.description: Perform code review on a file or path
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Perform a comprehensive code review on the following file or path. Check for correctness, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, and style problems.

Target:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-test
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Run the test suite and analyze results. If tests fail, identify the root cause and propose specific fixes.

Scope:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-implement
opencodeCommands.description: Implement a feature from a spec
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Implement the following feature or change according to the specification. Read existing code first and follow project conventions.

Spec:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-docs
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Generate or update documentation for the following target. Documentation must accurately reflect actual code behavior.

Target:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-explore
opencodeCommands.description: Map architecture, entrypoints, and ownership of a target
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Explore the following target. Map the entrypoints, major abstractions, neighboring modules, and likely change points. Keep the result evidence-first and scoped to understanding.

Target:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-trace
opencodeCommands.description: Trace a symbol, dependency edge, or data flow
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Trace the following symbol, file, or interface end-to-end. Report upstream callers or dependencies, downstream consumers or effects, and the main impact points.

Target:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-debug
opencodeCommands.description: Investigate a failure without drifting into implementation
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Debug the following failure or symptom. Reproduce the likely path from available evidence, narrow the root cause, and report the strongest verified or suspected explanation without changing code.

Failure:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-plan-feature
opencodeCommands.description: Break down a feature into tasks
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Break down the following feature into concrete implementation tasks. Include dependencies, risks, and complexity assessment.

Feature:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-plan-refactor
opencodeCommands.description: Plan a refactoring with impact analysis
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Plan the following refactoring. Analyze impact, identify affected files, and outline a migration path with rollback strategy.

Refactoring:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-audit
opencodeCommands.description: Security audit of a file, module, or API surface
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
Perform a security-focused audit of the following target. Prioritize: authentication gaps, input validation failures, injection vulnerabilities, secret exposure, authorization bypasses, and dependency CVEs. Every finding requires file:line citation and a specific fix. Flag BLOCKING issues that must be resolved before production deployment.

Target:

opencodeCommands.name: openagents-ship
opencodeCommands.promptTemplate:
End-to-end delivery of the following feature or fix:
1. Read current state and confirm understanding of the specification
2. Implement using @hephaestus
3. Run tests using @atalanta — do not proceed if tests fail
4. Review code using @nemesis — resolve all BLOCKING findings before proceeding
5. Update documentation using @calliope
6. Report: files changed, test status, review verdict, and any remaining manual steps
7. Remind the user to commit manually — auto-commit is forbidden

Feature or fix:

codexModes.prompt: Use openagentsbtw. Route this through hermes-style exploration on the utility profile. Keep the result concise, evidence-driven, and scoped to bounded search or classification.

Use openagentsbtw plus the explore skill. Route this through hermes-style repo exploration on the utility profile. Map architecture, entrypoints, ownership, and likely change points. Keep the result concise and evidence-driven.

Use openagentsbtw plus the trace skill. Route this through hermes-style tracing on the utility profile. Follow a symbol, dependency path, or data flow end-to-end and report upstream, downstream, and impact points.

Use openagentsbtw plus the debug skill. Route this through hermes-style investigation on the utility profile. Narrow the failure, separate verified evidence from suspicion, and do not drift into implementation.

Use openagentsbtw. Route this through hermes-style exploration on the utility profile. Use DeepWiki MCP tools first for public GitHub repository understanding, then verify exact local file or line claims with repo reads before stating them. Keep the result concise, evidence-driven, and scoped to exploration.
Use openagentsbtw. Route this through calliope-style documentation work on the utility profile. Keep prose factual, terse, and free of filler.
Use openagentsbtw. Route this through calliope plus the desloppify skill on the utility profile. Remove AI filler, sycophancy, narrating comments, and optional-offer boilerplate.
Use openagentsbtw. Route this through calliope for writing and hermes for exact repo state on the utility profile. Keep the summary compact and operational.
Resume a previous native Codex session with the managed openagentsbtw profile.

Use openagentsbtw. Route this through atalanta-style targeted validation on the utility profile. Run and analyze; do not drift into implementation. Completion requires actual execution evidence or a strict BLOCKED: line naming the concrete missing dependency or contradiction.

Use openagentsbtw. Route this through atalanta-style QA on the utility profile. Reproduce broadly, test neighboring variants instead of one narrow path, and separate verified failures from assumptions. Record exact repro steps, note passed and failed cases, prefer integration tests when behavior crosses boundaries, and use Playwright CLI for screenshots, traces, or DOM evidence when it is available. Completion requires actual execution evidence or a strict BLOCKED: line naming the concrete missing dependency or contradiction.

Use openagentsbtw. Route planning through athena-style architecture analysis on the main profile. Treat native /plan as reasoning mode only, not role selection. On Pro plans, default to multi-agent routing when it materially improves outcomes (disjoint ownership; avoid parallel edits). Use prompt contracts: explicit step order, explicit ask-vs-proceed rules, and crisp completion criteria. Lead with the recommendation and keep the plan concrete.

Use openagentsbtw. Route planning through athena-style architecture analysis on the main profile with Fast mode enabled (higher quota burn for speed). Treat native /plan as reasoning mode only, not role selection. On Pro plans, default to multi-agent routing when it materially improves outcomes (disjoint ownership; avoid parallel edits). Use prompt contracts: explicit step order, explicit ask-vs-proceed rules, and crisp completion criteria. Lead with the recommendation and keep the plan concrete.

Use openagentsbtw. Route implementation through hephaestus-style execution on the sandboxed auto-accept implementation profile. This mode keeps workspace-write sandboxing but does not pause for edit approvals. Prefer small cohesive diffs; avoid big rewrites and god-object files. Complete the requested work in real production code. Do not return scaffolding, prototypes, toy/demo code, educational side paths, or docs-only churn in place of the actual implementation. Avoid placeholders, avoid unnecessary fallbacks, and keep comments to non-obvious why. If the work cannot be completed, stop with a strict BLOCKED: line naming the concrete missing dependency or contradiction.

Use openagentsbtw. Route implementation through hephaestus-style execution on the implementation profile. On Pro plans, default to multi-agent routing when it materially improves outcomes (disjoint ownership; avoid parallel edits). Prefer small cohesive diffs; avoid big rewrites and god-object files. Complete the requested work in real production code. Do not return scaffolding, prototypes, toy/demo code, educational side paths, or docs-only churn in place of the actual implementation. Avoid placeholders, avoid unnecessary fallbacks, and keep comments to non-obvious why. If the work cannot be completed, stop with a strict BLOCKED: line naming the concrete missing dependency or contradiction.

Use openagentsbtw. Route implementation through hephaestus-style execution on the implementation profile with Fast mode enabled (higher quota burn for speed). On Pro plans, default to multi-agent routing when it materially improves outcomes (disjoint ownership; avoid parallel edits). Prefer small cohesive diffs; avoid big rewrites and god-object files. Complete the requested work in real production code. Do not return scaffolding, prototypes, toy/demo code, educational side paths, or docs-only churn in place of the actual implementation. Avoid placeholders, avoid unnecessary fallbacks, and keep comments to non-obvious why. If the work cannot be completed, stop with a strict BLOCKED: line naming the concrete missing dependency or contradiction.

Use openagentsbtw. Route review through nemesis-style analysis on the main profile. Lead with findings, cite evidence, and focus on correctness, regressions, security, and missing tests. Flag slop risk (god objects, muddy boundaries, over-broad diffs) as first-class review issues.

Use openagentsbtw. Route review through nemesis-style analysis on the main profile with Fast mode enabled (higher quota burn for speed). Lead with findings, cite evidence, and focus on correctness, regressions, security, and missing tests. Flag slop risk (god objects, muddy boundaries, over-broad diffs) as first-class review issues.

Use openagentsbtw. Route this through patient long-run execution on the longrun profile. For long-running builds, test suites, or validation jobs: start the command once, avoid killing a healthy process without concrete failure evidence, prefer coarse progress checks over impatient short polling, and do not invent fixes for a command that is merely still running. When RTK policy is active, prefer RTK-prefixed build and test commands. Completion requires actual execution evidence or a strict BLOCKED: line naming the concrete missing dependency or contradiction.

Use openagentsbtw. On Pro plans, spawn subagents by default for any non-trivial request (unless the user explicitly asks for single-agent work). On Go or Plus, delegate only when the payoff is clear. Assign disjoint ownership (paths/modules) per agent. Suggested split: hermes for repo exploration/tracing, hephaestus for edits, atalanta for tests, nemesis for review, calliope for docs. Wait for all agents and then produce one consolidated answer with a short section per agent plus next actions. This route is still an implementation route: complete the requested work in real production code, not prototypes, not scaffolding, not educational side paths. If the work cannot be completed, stop with a strict BLOCKED: line naming the concrete missing dependency or contradiction.

Use openagentsbtw. Route multi-step coordination through odysseus-style orchestration on the main profile. On Pro plans, default to multi-agent routing when it materially improves outcomes. Delegate only bounded work, assign disjoint ownership (paths/modules), avoid parallel edits unless scopes are separated, and track step ownership explicitly. This route is accountable for real completion: deliver the requested implementation in production code, not prototypes, not scaffolding, not educational side paths. If the work cannot be completed, stop with a strict BLOCKED: line naming the concrete missing dependency or contradiction.
